steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center and radius from the equation or graph the circle as a check. For diagram facts, mark equal radii, diameters, tangents, and arcs before doing algebra.

steps.title: Recall the standard form
steps.content: A circle in the $xy$-plane with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ has the equation $$(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2.$$ The right side is the radius squared, not the radius itself.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: $(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=49k$ — uses $49k$ instead of $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$. Incorrect.
  • Choice B: $(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=49k^2$ — matches $(7k)^2 = 49k^2$ exactly. Correct.
  • Choice C: $(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=7k$ — uses the radius, not its square. Incorrect.
  • Choice D: $(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=7k^2$ — only squares $k$, not the full radius $7k$. Incorrect.
That matches Choice B — $(x-14)^2+(y-19)^2=49k^2$.
